compiled_state.title: [Worktree Cleanup]: Branch merged via GitHub PR -> worktree not auto-deleted by claude-safe or worktree-cleanup.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

After a worktree's branch is merged to main via a GitHub Pull Request, `claude-safe`'s `_offer_worktree_cleanup` and `worktree-cleanup.sh`'s `is_branch_merged` should detect the branch as merged and offer/perform automatic deletion of the worktree and its local+remote branches.

#### Actual Behavior

Neither script detects the merge. `is_merged` stays 0. The worktree, local branch, and remote branch persist and must be deleted manually.

The `is_branch_merged` function uses two checks:
1. `git merge-base --is-ancestor "$branch" main` — fails for squash-merged and rebase-merged PRs (branch tip SHA is not an ancestor of main)
2. `git log main --grep="(merge $branch)"` — fails for GitHub PR merge commits whose messages follow the format `Merge pull request #N from org/branch` not `(merge branch)`

GitHub's default merge commit format `Merge pull request #N from navapbc/<branch>` is not matched by either check.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Two-file fix: plugins/dso/scripts/claude-safe (is_merged check) and plugins/dso/scripts/worktree-cleanup.sh (is_branch_merged function). Root: grep pattern '(merge $branch)' does not match GitHub PR merge commit format 'Merge pull request #N from org/branch'.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation RESULT: ROOT_CAUSE: The '(merge $branch)' grep pattern in _offer_worktree_cleanup (claude-safe) and is_branch_merged (worktree-cleanup.sh) only matches the internal merge-to-main.sh format, not GitHub PR merge commits ('Merge pull request #N from org/branch'). confidence: high. Proposed fix: add third fallback grep for branch name alone. Hypothesis tests: (1) existing pattern → no match (confirmed); (2) plain branch-name grep → match (confirmed); (3) gh pr list → works but network-dependent (confirmed). Gate 1a: intent-aligned (CLI_user tag). testing_mode=RED — no existing test covers GitHub PR merge commit format.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Anti-pattern scan complete: 0 confirmed candidates (pattern was specific to the two parallel is_merged check functions; no other occurrences found outside the fixed files).
comments.author: Test
